product_overview: PulseAPI is a simple real-time API uptime monitoring platform that lets users add HTTP endpoints, view uptime history and ping logs, receive email alerts, and share public status pages.
core_goals: Allow users to add, configure, and remove monitored HTTP endpoints with configurable check intervals.
Continuously record ping data (status code, response time, timestamp) and surface current UP/DOWN status and 30-day uptime.
Send email alerts on DOWN and recovery transitions with a user-controllable toggle.
Provide a dashboard and activity logs for troubleshooting and shareable public status pages for stakeholders.
Support simple authentication flows: email/password signup with OTP verification and Google OAuth.

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public home page presenting product value, FAQ, and primary calls-to-action to sign up or go to the dashboard.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> View hero section with product description -> Expand/collapse FAQ accordion items -> Click CTA button -> Navigate to /signup
Navigate to / -> View hero section with product description -> Click CTA button while signed-in -> Navigate to /dashboard
Navigate to / -> Expand FAQ accordion item -> Collapse FAQ accordion item -> See FAQ content hide/show

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticate using email/password or Google OAuth and access the authenticated dashboard; recover access via forgot password.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> See dashboard at /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Click Google sign-in button for OAuth login -> Complete provider consent -> Redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> See error message on invalid credentials
Navigate to /login -> Click forgot password link -> Navigate to /forgot-password

features.name: User Signup
features.description: Register with name, email and password or via Google OAuth; leads to OTP-based email verification.
user_flows: Navigate to /signup -> Fill name input field -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Signup button to submit -> Navigate to /verify-email
Navigate to /signup -> Click Google sign-up button for OAuth -> Complete provider consent -> Redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /signup -> Fill name input field -> Fill invalid email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Signup button to submit -> See validation errors

features.name: Email Verification
features.description: OTP-based verification for newly registered users before granting full access.
user_flows: Navigate to /verify-email -> Enter OTP code received via email -> Click verify button -> See success message -> Navigate to /dashboard
Navigate to /verify-email -> Enter incorrect OTP code -> Click verify button -> See error message about invalid OTP
Navigate to /verify-email -> Click resend code button -> See confirmation that verification OTP was resent

features.name: Forgot Password
features.description: Request a password reset via email OTP and set a new password using the received OTP.
user_flows: Navigate to /forgot-password -> Enter email address -> Click send reset code button -> See instruction that OTP was sent -> Enter OTP code -> Enter new password -> Click reset password button -> See success confirmation
Navigate to /forgot-password -> Enter email address -> Click send reset code button -> Enter invalid OTP code -> Enter new password -> Click reset password button -> See error message for invalid OTP
Navigate to /forgot-password -> Enter email address -> Click send reset code button -> Enter new password that fails validation -> Click reset password button -> See password validation error

features.name: Endpoint Dashboard
features.description: Authenticated dashboard showing monitored endpoints, their current status, and controls to add or delete endpoints with auto-refresh.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> View list of all monitored endpoints with status indicators -> Fill add endpoint form name field -> Fill add endpoint form URL field -> Select HTTP method -> Select check interval -> Click add button to create new endpoint -> See new endpoint appear in list
Navigate to /dashboard -> View list of all monitored endpoints -> Click delete button on an endpoint to remove it -> Confirm deletion -> See endpoint removed from list
Navigate to /dashboard -> View list of all monitored endpoints -> Wait for auto-refresh (every 15s) -> See real-time status updates reflected in list
Navigate to /dashboard -> Fill add endpoint form name field -> Fill add endpoint form with invalid URL -> Click add button to create new endpoint -> See validation error for URL

features.name: Activity Logs
features.description: Detailed ping history and uptime visualization per endpoint, with the ability to share a public status page link.
user_flows: Navigate to /logs -> Select an endpoint from dropdown -> View endpoint health panel (uptime %, status, config) -> View 30-day uptime bar chart -> View recent ping history table (last 20 entries) -> Click share button to copy public status page URL
Navigate to /logs -> Select an endpoint from dropdown -> Click share button -> See copied URL confirmation
Navigate to /logs -> No endpoints available -> See empty state message prompting to add endpoints

features.name: User Settings
features.description: Display user profile information and allow toggling of email alert preferences.
user_flows: Navigate to /settings -> View profile name and email (read-only) -> Toggle email alerts switch on -> See visual confirmation and saved state
Navigate to /settings -> Toggle email alerts switch off -> See visual confirmation and saved state
Navigate to /settings -> Toggle email alerts switch -> API update fails -> See error message and reverted toggle state

features.name: Public Status Page
features.description: A publicly accessible page for a shared endpoint showing current status, uptime chart, and recent pings using a share token.
user_flows: Navigate to /status/:shareToken -> View endpoint name, URL, and current status -> View 30-day uptime bar chart -> View recent ping history table
Navigate to /status/:invalidShareToken -> See public status not found or 404 message

routes.description: Landing page with features overview, FAQ, and CTA
User login page with email/password and Google OAuth
User registration page with email/password and Google OAuth
OTP email verification page after signup
Main dashboard showing all monitored endpoints with add/delete functionality
Activity logs with ping history, uptime bar, and share button
User settings page with email alerts toggle
Public status page for a shared endpoint
